spans.content: Autonomous driving systems require an understanding of map elements on the road,including
lanes,pedestriancrossing,and traffic signs,to navigatearound the world.
In contrast, we focus on using onboard sensors, including LiDARs and cameras,to estimate map
·We utilize polyline to accommodate the heterogeneous nature of map elements.
·Our method achieves promising performance in online semantic HD map learning tasks.
Similar to HDMapNet,our task is to generate vectorized map elements using data from onboard
sensors of autonomous vehicle,such as RGB cameras and/or LiDARs.These map elements include:
·Lane dividers:boundaries dividing lanes on the road,usually straight lines.
Road boundaries:boundaries of roads separating roadsand sidewalks,typicallyiregularly-shaped
·Pedestrian crossings: regions with white markings indicating legal pedestrian crossing points,
·Centerlines:line segments that commonly used for driving direction,vehicle positioning,and
Why use polyline to represent maps?
Using polylines to represent map elements has three main advantages:
·Polylines are a flexible primitive thatcan represent complex geometric elements in HD maps.
·Theorder of polyline vertices isa natural way to encode thedirection of map elements.
·The polyline representation has been widely used bydownstream tasks,such as motion forecasting.
